speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] Right, let's dive into our green segment with some absolutely magnificent news from our oceans. The United Nations Environment Programme has just announced that we've hit a massive milestone - 10% of the world's oceans are now officially under protection!

speaker: charlie
text: [Australian accent] That's incredible, Jamie! We're talking about 5 million square kilometres of ocean protected just in the last two years alone - that's bigger than the entire European Union. It's like the ocean equivalent of creating massive national parks under the waves!

speaker: alice
text: [Australian accent] And speaking of conservation wins, Japan has officially removed the red-crowned crane from their threatened species list! These beautiful birds went from feared extinct in the early 1900s to nearly 2,000 birds today. Sometimes called symbols of good fortune, they're certainly bringing us good news today!
